ddls:
CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_type" TEXT /* Type of the node (e.g., EDGE) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the node (e.g., INIT, ASSIGNED, OPTIMIZED) */,
  "sar_dl" REAL /* Signal-to-air interface (SAR) for downlink (DL) communications */,
  "sar_ul" REAL /* Signal-to-air interface (SAR) for uplink (UL) communications */,
  "amc" INTEGER /* Adaptive modulation and coding (AMC) scheme used */,
  "rb_used" INTEGER /* Number of resource blocks (RB) used by the node */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical coordinates of the node */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum capacity of the node */,
  "operational_hours" INTEGER /* Operational hours of the node */,
  "maintenance_status" TEXT /* Current maintenance status of the node */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection */,
  "node_a_id" INTEGER /* ID of node A in the connection */,
  "node_b_id" INTEGER /* ID of node B in the connection */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance between the two nodes */,
  "is_los" INTEGER /* Whether the connection has line-of-sight (LOS) */,
  "path_loss" REAL /* Path loss of the connection */,
  "max_path_loss" REAL /* Maximum path loss of the connection */,
  "bitrate" INTEGER /* Bitrate of the connection */,
  "max_bitrate" INTEGER /* Maximum bitrate of the connection */,
  "is_assignable" INTEGER /* Whether the connection is assignable */,
  "signal_strength" REAL /* Current signal strength of the connection */,
  "quality_of_service" REAL /* Quality of service measure of the connection */,
  "data_volume" INTEGER /* Amount of data transferred through the connection */,
  "connection_type" TEXT /* Type of the connection (e.g., fiber, wireless) */,
  "last_optimized" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last optimization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_node_b_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_b_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connections_node_a_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_a_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)
column_names: Status of the node (e.g., INIT, ASSIGNED, OPTIMIZED)
Adaptive modulation and coding (AMC) scheme used
Number of resource blocks (RB) used by the node
Current maintenance status of the node
ID of node A in the connection
ID of node B in the connection
Maximum path loss of the connection
Current signal strength of the connection
Quality of service measure of the connection
Amount of data transferred through the connection
Type of the connection (e.g., fiber, wireless)
